news.title: Non-VR Arizona Sunshine
news.text:
Vertigo Games announces plans for a new version of Arizona Sunshine 
that will be playable on PC and consoles via traditional flat screens. This 
zombie-infested first-person shooter first had its day in the sun 
back in 2016 as a VR-only game, so this 
will bring it to a wider audience. Listings are live for the Windows edition on 
the Epic Games 

saying this is coming "soon. The
Reveal Trailer takes a 
look at gameplay, showing what's revealed in the announcement, which is this 
will add the chance to play as Buddy, the game's best boy:Built on the 
foundation of the series’ signature brutal gameplay and the frantic but 
surprisingly touching story of Arizona Sunshine VR 2, Arizona Sunshine brings 
brand-new ways to survive the heart-pounding, limb-severing and dark-humored 
apocalypse. The game features narrative-driven solo play and chaotic co-op where 
every encounter can spiral out of control, all in an accessible third-person 
perspective on PC and consoles.

It’s hot, it’s bloody and frankly, you smell terrible. Meet Sunny, a 
dark-humored survivor who has spent way too much time alone in the desert 
talking to the undead he calls “Fred." Together with his fiercely loyal, 
zombie-chomping dog Buddy, he sets out on a sun-drenched, gore-splattered road 
trip across Arizona. It’s a witty, frantic story about survival, loneliness, and 
the bond between a man and his dog.

Sure, surviving alone is fine, but why not share the trauma? Grab a friend, a 
pizza and a controller for chaotic split-screen co-op, or jump into a two-player 
zombie-slaying sesh online. Blast through massive hordes using fan-favorite 
weapons, from shotguns and machetes to flamethrowers and more. Firefights break 
down fast, split-second saves become routine and nothing ever goes according to
news.author: Stephen Heaslip

news.title: FCC Applications for KTRK-TV
news.text:
HOUSTON -- To learn about FCC applications regarding KTRK-TV, Channel 13 (RF Channel 13) Houston, TX click HERE

On May 28, 2026, KTRK Television, Inc., licensee of KTRK-TV, Channel 13 (RF Channel 13), Houston, Texas, filed an application with the Federal Communications Commission for renewal of its broadcast license. Members of the public wishing to view this application or obtain information about how to file comments and petitions on the application can visit publicfiles.fcc.gov.
news.summary: On May 28, 2026, KTRK Television, Inc., licensee of KTRK-TV, filed an application with the FCC for renewal of its license.
news.author: Houston

news.title: FCC Applications for KGO-TV
news.text:
SAN FRANCISCO -- To learn about FCC applications regarding KGO-TV, Channel 7 (RF Channel 12) San Francisco, California click HERE.

On May 28, 2026, KGO Television, Inc., licensee of KGO-TV, Channel 7 (RF Channel 12), San Francisco, California, filed an application with the Federal Communications Commission for renewal of its broadcast license. Members of the public wishing to view this application or obtain information about how to file comments and petitions on the application can visit publicfiles.fcc.gov.
news.summary: On May 28, 2026, KGO Television, Inc., licensee of KGO-TV, Channel 7 (RF Channel 12) San Francisco, California, filed an application with the Federal Communications Commission for renewal of its license.
news.author: San Francisco
